2026-04-23T04:13:38.6537817Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T04:13:38.6538357Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T04:13:38.6538776Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T04:13:38.6539137Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T04:13:38.6539448Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T04:13:38.6539685Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T04:13:38.6539843Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-23T04:13:38.6540190Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m

Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. 2026-04-23T04:16:28.3445498Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T04:14:30.276556755Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58548,\"title\":\"[DnsResolver] Docs: README missing key concepts and code examples after TypeSpec migration\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/dnsresolver/Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57068 (commit 34ad0de) by `@live1206`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57068 migrated `Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver` from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec-based generation. The migration added numerous new Custom source files exposing resource types including `DnsResolverResource`, `DnsForwardingRulesetResource`, `DnsResolverPolicyResource`, `DnsResolverDomainListResource`, `DnsResolverInboundEndpointResource`, `DnsResolverOutboundEndpointResource`, and `DnsForwardingRulesetVirtualNetworkLinkResource`. The CHANGELOG notes the TypeSpec migration but the README was not updated to reflect the expanded resource surface.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Missing Key Concepts section content:** The `## Key concepts` section only links to generic Azure SDK docs; it does not describe the specific resource types (`DnsResolver`, `DnsForwardingRuleset`, `DnsResolverPolicy`, `DnsResolverDomainList`, inbound/outbound endpoints) or their relationships\\n- **No inline code examples:** The `## Examples` section only links to an external samples page with no embedded code snippets showing how to create a DNS resolver, configure forwarding rulesets, manage domain lists, or set up inbound/outbound endpoints\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` directory:** There are no snippet test files (e.g., `SampleXxx.cs`) that the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling would extract to populate README code blocks\\n- **README does not mention new `DnsResolverDomainList` and `DnsResolverPolicy` resources** introduced in the 1.2.0-beta.1 / 1.3.0-beta.1 releases, which are key additions in this package generation\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify README.md\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/dnsresolver/Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace the `## Key concepts` section and the `## Examples` section with the content below.\\n\\nReplace the existing `## Key concepts` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library manages the following Azure DNS Private Resolver resource types:\\n\\n- **`DnsResolverResource`** – A DNS private resolver attached to a virtual network that enables name resolution between on-premises networks and Azure private DNS zones.\\n- **`DnsResolverInboundEndpointResource`** – An IP endpoint inside the resolver\\u0026#39;s VNet that accepts DNS queries from on-premises (or other networks) forwarded to Azure DNS.\\n- **`DnsResolverOutboundEndpointResource`** – An endpoint used by DNS forwarding rulesets to send queries to on-premises DNS servers.\\n- **`DnsForwardingRulesetResource`** – A set of DNS forwarding rules that redirect queries for specific domain names to on-premises or custom DNS servers via outbound endpoints.\\n- **`DnsForwardingRuleResource`** – An individual forwarding rule within a ruleset that maps a domain name to one or more target DNS servers.\\n- **`DnsForwardingRulesetVirtualNetworkLinkResource`** – Links a forwarding ruleset to a virtual network so that VMs in that VNet use the ruleset\\u0026#39;s rules.\\n- **`DnsResolverPolicyResource`** – A DNS security policy that can block or allow DNS queries based on domain lists.\\n- **`DnsResolverDomainListResource`** – A list of domain names referenced by DNS security policy rules (including managed threat-intelligence lists such as `AzureDnsThreatIntel`).\\n\\nFor a conceptual overview see [What is Azure DNS Private Resolver?]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the existing `## Examples` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples demonstrate common operations for the DNS Private Resolver management library.\\n\\n### Create a DNS Resolver\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create an Inbound Endpoint\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a Forwarding Ruleset\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Add a Forwarding Rule\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor additional code samples, see the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add sample test file\\n\\nCreate a new file at `sdk/dnsresolver/Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver/tests/Samples/Sample1_DnsResolver.cs` using this template (fill in real subscription/resource IDs with test-framework equivalents from `DnsResolverTestBase`):\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Network;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Network.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_DnsResolver : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateDnsResolver()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateDnsResolver\\n            // Authenticate and get the default subscription\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            // Create a resource group\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = (await subscription.GetResourceGroups()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;,\\n                    new ResourceGroupData(AzureLocation.EastUS))).Value;\\n\\n            // Reference an existing virtual network\\n            ResourceIdentifier vnetId = new ResourceIdentifier(\\n                $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscription.Id.SubscriptionId}/resourceGroups/myResourceGroup/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/myVNet\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            // Create the DNS resolver\\n            DnsResolverCollection resolvers = resourceGroup.GetDnsResolvers();\\n            DnsResolverData resolverData = new DnsResolverData(AzureLocation.EastUS)\\n            {\\n                VirtualNetwork = new WritableSubResource { Id = vnetId }\\n            };\\n            DnsResolverResource resolver = (await resolvers.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myDnsResolver\\u0026#34;, resolverData)).Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created DNS Resolver: {resolver.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateInboundEndpoint()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateInboundEndpoint\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DnsResolverResource resolver = await resourceGroup.GetDnsResolvers()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myDnsResolver\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ResourceIdentifier subnetId = new ResourceIdentifier(\\n                $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscription.Id.SubscriptionId}/resourceGroups/myResourceGroup/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/myVNet/subnets/inboundSubnet\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DnsResolverInboundEndpointData inboundData = new DnsResolverInboundEndpointData(\\n                AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n                new List\\n                {\\n                    new InboundEndpointIPConfiguration(new WritableSubResource { Id = subnetId })\\n                });\\n\\n            DnsResolverInboundEndpointResource inboundEndpoint = (await resolver.GetDnsResolverInboundEndpoints()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myInboundEndpoint\\u0026#34;, inboundData)).Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created Inbound Endpoint: {inboundEndpoint.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ResourceIdentifier outboundEndpointId = new ResourceIdentifier(\\n                $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscription.Id.SubscriptionId}/resourceGroups/myResourceGroup/providers/Microsoft.Network/dnsResolvers/myDnsResolver/outboundEndpoints/myOutboundEndpoint\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DnsForwardingRulesetData rulesetData = new DnsForwardingRulesetData(\\n                AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n                new List { new WritableSubResource { Id = outboundEndpointId } });\\n\\n            DnsForwardingRulesetResource ruleset = (await resourceGroup.GetDnsForwardingRulesets()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myForwardingRuleset\\u0026#34;, rulesetData)).Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created Forwarding Ruleset: {ruleset.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateForwardingRule()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateForwardingRule\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DnsForwardingRulesetResource ruleset = await resourceGroup.GetDnsForwardingRulesets()\\n               targetDnsServers: new List\\n                {\\n                    new TargetDnsServer(\\u0026#34;10.0.0.4\\u0026#34;, 53)\\n                });\\n\\n            DnsForwardingRuleResource rule = (await ruleset.GetDnsForwardingRules()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myForwardingRule\\u0026#34;, ruleData)).Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created Forwarding Rule: {rule.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe The migration added numerous new Custom source files exposing resource types including `DnsResolverResource`, `DnsForwardingRulesetResource`, `DnsResolverPolicyResource`, `DnsResolverDomainListResource`, `DnsResolverInboundEndpointResource`, `DnsResolverOutboundEndpointResource`, and `DnsForwardingRulesetVirtualNetworkLinkResource`. The CHANGELOG notes the TypeSpec migration but the README was not updated to reflect the expanded resource surface.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Missing Key Concepts section content:** The `## Key concepts` section only links to generic Azure SDK docs; it does not describe the specific resource types (`DnsResolver`, `DnsForwardingRuleset`, `DnsResolverPolicy`, `DnsResolverDomainList`, inbound/outbound endpoints) or their relationships\\n- **No inline code examples:** The `## Examples` section only links to an external samples page with no embedded code snippets showing how to create a DNS resolver, configure forwarding rulesets, manage domain lists, or set up inbound/outbound endpoints\\n- **No `tests/Samples/` directory:** There are no snippet test files (e.g., `SampleXxx.cs`) that the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling would extract to populate README code blocks\\n- **README does not mention new `DnsResolverDomainList` and `DnsResolverPolicy` resources** introduced in the 1.2.0-beta.1 / 1.3.0-beta.1 releases, which are key additions in this package generation\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify README.md\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/dnsresolver/Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace the `## Key concepts` section and the `## Examples` section with the content below.\\n\\nReplace the existing `## Key concepts` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library manages the following Azure DNS Private Resolver resource types:\\n\\n- **`DnsResolverResource`** – A DNS private resolver attached to a virtual network that enables name resolution between on-premises networks and Azure private DNS zones.\\n- **`DnsResolverInboundEndpointResource`** – An IP endpoint inside the resolver\\u0026#39;s VNet that accepts DNS queries from on-premises (or other networks) forwarded to Azure DNS.\\n- **`DnsResolverOutboundEndpointResource`** – An endpoint used by DNS forwarding rulesets to send queries to on-premises DNS servers.\\n- **`DnsForwardingRulesetResource`** – A set of DNS forwarding rules that redirect queries for specific domain names to on-premises or custom DNS servers via outbound endpoints.\\n- **`DnsForwardingRuleResource`** – An individual forwarding rule within a ruleset that maps a domain name to one or more target DNS servers.\\n- **`DnsForwardingRulesetVirtualNetworkLinkResource`** – Links a forwarding ruleset to a virtual network so that VMs in that VNet use the ruleset\\u0026#39;s rules.\\n- **`DnsResolverPolicyResource`** – A DNS security policy that can block or allow DNS queries based on domain lists.\\n- **`DnsResolverDomainListResource`** – A list of domain names referenced by DNS security policy rules (including managed threat-intelligence lists such as `AzureDnsThreatIntel`).\\n\\nFor a conceptual overview see [What is Azure DNS Private Resolver?]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nReplace the existing `## Examples` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples demonstrate common operations for the DNS Private Resolver management library.\\n\\n### Create a DNS Resolver\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create an Inbound Endpoint\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a Forwarding Ruleset\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Add a Forwarding Rule\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor additional code samples, see the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add sample test file\\n\\nCreate a new file at `sdk/dnsresolver/Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver/tests/Samples/Sample1_DnsResolver.cs` using this template (fill in real subscription/resource IDs with test-framework equivalents from `DnsResolverTestBase`):\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Network;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Network.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.DnsResolver.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_DnsResolver : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateDnsResolver()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateDnsResolver\\n            // Authenticate and get the default subscription\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            // Create a resource group\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = (await subscription.GetResourceGroups()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;,\\n                    new ResourceGroupData(AzureLocation.EastUS))).Value;\\n\\n            // Reference an existing virtual network\\n            ResourceIdentifier vnetId = new ResourceIdentifier(\\n                $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscription.Id.SubscriptionId}/resourceGroups/myResourceGroup/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/myVNet\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            // Create the DNS resolver\\n            DnsResolverCollection resolvers = resourceGroup.GetDnsResolvers();\\n            DnsResolverData resolverData = new DnsResolverData(AzureLocation.EastUS)\\n            {\\n                VirtualNetwork = new WritableSubResource { Id = vnetId }\\n            };\\n            DnsResolverResource resolver = (await resolvers.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myDnsResolver\\u0026#34;, resolverData)).Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created DNS Resolver: {resolver.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateInboundEndpoint()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateInboundEndpoint\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DnsResolverResource resolver = await resourceGroup.GetDnsResolvers()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myDnsResolver\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ResourceIdentifier subnetId = new ResourceIdentifier(\\n                $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscription.Id.SubscriptionId}/resourceGroups/myResourceGroup/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/myVNet/subnets/inboundSubnet\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DnsResolverInboundEndpointData inboundData = new DnsResolverInboundEndpointData(\\n                AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n                new List\\n                {\\n                    new InboundEndpointIPConfiguration(new WritableSubResource { Id = subnetId })\\n                });\\n\\n            DnsResolverInboundEndpointResource inboundEndpoint = (await resolver.GetDnsResolverInboundEndpoints()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myInboundEndpoint\\u0026#34;, inboundData)).Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created Inbound Endpoint: {inboundEndpoint.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateForwardingRuleset()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateForwardingRuleset\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ResourceIdentifier outboundEndpointId = new ResourceIdentifier(\\n                $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscription.Id.SubscriptionId}/resourceGroups/myResourceGroup/providers/Microsoft.Network/dnsResolvers/myDnsResolver/outboundEndpoints/myOutboundEndpoint\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DnsForwardingRulesetData rulesetData = new DnsForwardingRulesetData(\\n                AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n                new List { new WritableSubResource { Id = outboundEndpointId } });\\n\\n            DnsForwardingRulesetResource ruleset = (await resourceGroup.GetDnsForwardingRulesets()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myForwardingRuleset\\u0026#34;, rulesetData)).Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created Forwarding Ruleset: {ruleset.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateForwardingRule()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateForwardingRule\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroups()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DnsForwardingRulesetResource ruleset = await resourceGroup.GetDnsForwardingRulesets()\\n                .GetAsync(\\u0026#34;myForwardingRuleset\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            DnsForwardingRuleData ruleData = new DnsForwardingRuleData(\\n                domainName: \\u0026#34;contoso.com.\\u0026#34;,\\n                targetDnsServers: new List\\n                {\\n                    new TargetDnsServer(\\u0026#34;10.0.0.4\\u0026#34;, 53)\\n                });\\n\\n            DnsForwardingRuleResource rule = (await ruleset.GetDnsForwardingRules()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myForwardingRule\\u0026#34;, ruleData)).Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created Forwarding Rule: {rule.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe
